Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID J. MILLSTEIN OF MILLSTEIN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 348-0348 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GEORGE J. ZISER OF LEWIS BRISBOIS, TEL. NO. 438-6659 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Motion For Preliminary Injunction is Granted. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association is enjoined from implementing the result of the July 25, 2014 member election until a trial on the merits is held. Plaintiff has established a likelihood of success. The evidence shows that Defendant included a letter advocating for the passage of the amendment with the ballot, without providing Plaintiff equal opportunity to include literature opposing the amendment, in violation of Civ. Code sec. 5105(a)(1). (Declaration of Kazuko Artus, pars. 25-29, Ex. M.) This unfairly gave an appearance of legitimacy to Defendant's position on the Amendment. (See e.g. Knoll v. Davidson (1974) 12 Cal.3d 335, 352.) The evidence also shows that Defendant used association funds to include the letter with the ballot materials, in violation of Civ. Code sec. 5135(a). (Artus Decl., pars. 25-29, Ex. M.) Plaintiff has established that the balance of harms weighs in her favor. If the injunction is not issued, a director's election would proceed in January implementing a procedure that resulted from an election that did not comply with the Davis-Sterling Act. The injunction merely maintains the status quo until a trial on the merits may be held. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
